actions.text: ## Key Decisions and Agreements
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Exit-focused strategy within one year** - The team has decided to pivot from ongoing operations to actively pursuing acquisition within 12 months. Rationale: Current runway constraints combined with uncertain revenue trajectory make a strategic exit more viable than continued fundraising. This matters because it fundamentally shifts priorities from product development to positioning for acquisition.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Team restructuring to lean operations** - Decision to reduce US-based overhead and shift work to India team to extend runway. Specific changes: Rockle not needed immediately, Ilsa potentially part-time. This preserves cash while maintaining essential technical capabilities for sale preparation.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Explore roll-up model positioning** - Investigate framing Careerspan as a recruiting agency roll-up company rather than pure tech play. The idea: Position as acquiring and modernizing traditional recruiting firms, leveraging Careerspan tech to improve recruiter-to-candidate ratios and profitability. This matters because it opens access to different investor pools (PE-backed startups targeting archaic industries) and creates a bigger fundraising narrative.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **$5M acquisition target established** - Agreed on $5M as the minimum viable exit that provides meaningful returns: ~$1.3M for Vrijen, ~$600K for Logan, ~$400K for Ilsa. This concrete target enables working backwards to identify specific acquisition scenarios and prepare appropriate proof points.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **War-game acquisition targets next week** - Logan and Vrijen to meet in Boston (Monday or Tuesday) to map acquisition landscape systematically: who has deep pockets, what are they acquiring for (tech vs. distribution), what proof points are needed for each scenario.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: **Apply to Work Tech Sherm** - Following up on recent call and Laura's recommendation. Deadline approaching, need to coordinate with team on application.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: ## Strategic Context
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Market Positioning Tension:** The conversation reveals a core strategic question about whether to sell on technical capabilities (AI verification, background checks, resume-to-interview consistency) or distribution strength (embedded in communities, user volume, unique data access). The tech angle is harder to make objective cases for but potentially higher value; distribution is easier to demonstrate but requires more community partnerships.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Runway Reality:** Without additional capital, the team needs to make hard choices. The "leaky bucket" concern (putting money into a business without proven revenue) is driving the exit-focused approach rather than raising more funds. This is pragmatic but also reflects market conditions where career tech consolidation is accelerating.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Roll-Up Model Opportunity:** Vrijen's observation about Google Ventures/Compadre Capital showcasing three roll-up companies (targeting accountants, business automation) suggests a viable fundraising path exists for companies acquiring and modernizing legacy industries. The recruiting industry fits this pattern: fragmented firms with proprietary networks and qualitative judgment that could be centralized and tech-enabled.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: AI Democratization Paradox:** Interesting meta-observation that LLMs are "pseudo-democratizing" rather than truly democratizing - powerful enough to 4x capability for those who can wield them (like Vrijen with Zo Computer), but still requiring significant effort/sophistication. This creates a temporary advantage window for technical founders who can bridge the gap.
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Community vs. Enterprise Pathways:** Two viable positioning strategies emerged:
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: 1. Consumer/community play (distribution strength, embedded relationships, user volume)
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: 2. Enterprise tech play (background verification, AI-powered hiring tools, API/SDK integrations)
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: The former plays to Vrijen and Logan's networking strengths; the latter requires more technical validation but opens doors to companies like Checker (which just made a $2B acquisition in verification space).
actions.scheduling_reasoning: Normal priority tasks scheduled in 13-16:00 window

actions.text: Purpose:** Systematically identify all potential acquirers segmented by what they would buy for (tech capability vs. distribution/data), research their acquisition history, map proof points needed for each scenario, and create outreach strategy leveraging existing networks. This provides the roadmap for next 12 months of positioning work.
